fault_hypothesis: The overlap detection logic defined on line 72 is incorrect because it uses non-strict inequalities (`<=`, `>=`), failing to treat adjacent (touching) time slots as overlapping. This incorrect logic is used in the `if` condition on line 76, causing the program to incorrectly accept adjacent time slots.

spec.expr: not (effective[1] == candidate[0] or candidate[1] == effective[0]) or (not (candidate[1] <= effective[0] or candidate[0] >= effective[1]))

constraints.intent: If two time slots are adjacent (touching), they must be considered overlapping.

spec.expr: not (effective[1] == candidate[0] or candidate[1] == effective[0]) or (not (candidate[1] <= effective[0] or candidate[0] >= effective[1]))

constraints.intent: During each iteration, the overlap check must correctly handle adjacent slots.

spec.expr: not any(s[1] == candidate[0] or s[0] == candidate[1] for s in existing)

constraints.intent: A candidate should not be merged into the schedule if it is merely adjacent to an existing slot.

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] and any(s[1] == candidate[0] or s[0] == candidate[1] for s in existing))

constraints.intent: If a candidate is accepted, it must not be adjacent to any previously existing time slot.
